$comment: c84 D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT (the SectionB-locked option A — ELF COPY relocations): stdout/stderr/stdin ship from stdio.json as extern DATA objects (kind:object, FILE*, availableObjectFormats:[elf]); the ELF exec walker reserves a .bss copy slot per stream, exports each as a DEFINED OBJECT dynsym (st_size=8 from the DataModel pointer width, NEVER hardcoded) and emits R_X86_64_COPY(5)/R_AARCH64_COPY(1024) in .rela.dyn; ld-linux copies libc's FILE* values in before entry. The runner's capture MERGES the child's stderr into capturedStdout, so expectedStdout asserts BOTH streams' bytes in a DETERMINISTIC order (stdout explicitly fflush'd before the stderr write; C 7.21.3p7 keeps stderr un-fully-buffered) — proving both FILE* slots bind the real libc streams; the 1>/2> SEPARATION is asserted by the manual WSL validation (a merged capture cannot express it). Both fprintf return values (22 chars each) feed the exit code; the three slots must be non-null + pairwise-distinct. ELF LEGS ONLY: pe/macho do not ship the objects (their dataImportBinding models have not landed — a reference there stays an honest S0001; the linker rejects any surviving data import loud). RED-ON-DISABLE witnessed: un-ship the objects -> S0001; drop dataImportBinding from the format -> K_FormatLacksImportSupport at link. gcc -std=c17 -no-pie cross-checked (exit 42, identical stream bytes, same COPY mechanism).
